title: Algebraic Structure, Equations, and Inequalities · Decision Notebook: Parameter Sensitivity
summary: Build a tariff and break-even calculator with piecewise constraints. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int parameter sensitivity increment in an audited tariff and break-even model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Analyze how parameter changes move roots, intersections, and feasible ranges.
Solve and graph compound and two-variable inequality systems as feasible regions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate Parameter Sensitivity evidence
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 22 units, changes by −9 units, then by +8 units. Calculate the final quantity. Use the result to decide Parameter Sensitivity for an audited tariff and break-even model.
steps.why: The Parameter Sensitivity result for an audited tariff and break-even model is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 22 − 9 + 8; compare with the starting scale. Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Analyze how parameter changes move roots, intersections, and feasible ranges.
